text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check small, individual parts of the code, like a single function. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different modules work together correctly. In test-driven development (TDD), developers write tests before the code itself. This approach guides the design and ensures every feature is testable from the start. Together, these practices build confidence. When you have a strong suite of tests, you can make changes or add new features without fear of breaking existing functionality. The tests act as a safety net, proving that the system still works as expected.
